data.title:
$BTC Weekend so not expecting much in terms of BTC.

For the first time in weeks, we actually saw BTC rally at the end of the week on Friday. Most previous weeks we saw a rally into Friday only to fall right before the weekend. It was the opposite way around now.

We'll see where…
In 2018, ETH whales sold 4,245 ETH again in one week
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, in 2018, the ETH whale sold 4,245 ETH again after a week, worth 9.44 million US dollars. Since February 15, the cumulative amount of ETH sold has reached 16,499 ETH (42.92 million US dollars). The address recharged ETH to Binance 8 hours ago and was suspected of selling. Its ETH cost was as low as $194. If it was sold, it would make a profit of 8.62 million US dollars.

data.title: The CEBG ecological token CEC of the chain game has increased by as much as 610% in 24 hours and is currently trading at US$0.034.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to GMGN data, the CEC token of the blockchain game CEBG ecosystem has a maximum increase of 610% in 24 hours and is now reported at $0.034.
It is reported that CEBG announced its AI+game strategy on Friday, and will launch an AI Gaming middle layer based on AI and game communication protocols to provide core services such as intelligent generation, AI chain reasoning, and instant messaging for various games. It is planned to land on the first game within March, and at the same time upgrade PvAI narrative promotion.

data.title: Yuga Labs Co-Founder Highlights Spoofing Concerns in Punks Market
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on March 2, Yuga Labs co-founder Greg Solano reported that there are instances of individuals using flash loans to create fake bids in the Punks market. This practice, known as spoofing, involves manipulating market activity to deceive participants.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin changed his Twitter profile picture to a character from the game "World of Warcraft"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Vitalik Buterin changed his Twitter account profile to a creature from World of Warcraft, and the Twitter account profile is "I choose balance. First-level balance. mi pinxe lo crino tcati". This character is a druid in the game and has a talent for balance. Previously, Vitalik's Twitter profile was the Milady series NFT.

data.title: Upbit will launch PUNDIAI trading pair on March 2
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Upbit announced that it will officially launch PUNDIAI token trading. According to the official announcement, PUNDIAI will be available for trading at 10:00 Beijing time on March 2, 2025.
PUNDIAI is an artificial intelligence-enhanced payment solution platform that focuses on combining AI with blockchain technology to provide users with a smarter payment experience. Previously, PUNDIAI has been listed on multiple exchanges, and this listing on Upbit will further expand its market coverage.

data.title: New Proposal Aims to Democratize Ethereum's Block Building Process
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a new proposal has been introduced by an Ethereum researcher, aiming to decentralize the current block building process on the Ethereum network. The proposal, named 'Decentralized Random Block Proposal,' seeks to implement a shared random algorithm to democratize block proposing and eliminate Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) at the block level. This approach leverages Byzantine Fault Tolerance (BFT) to potentially accelerate the propagation of transactions to network validators, as explained by the pseudonymous researcher Malik672 in a recent post.

Malik672 highlighted that while Ethereum's proof-of-stake model and the proposer-builder separation feature have somewhat reduced the concentration of MEV, they have also led to increased centralization among builders and relays. Ethereum Foundation researcher Toni Wahrstätter noted that two block builders, Beaverbuild and Titan Builder, were responsible for constructing 88.7% of all Ethereum blocks in the first half of October. Although this figure has decreased to around 80%, it remains higher than desired by the Ethereum community.

The proposed system aims to distribute block building across thousands of clients globally, thereby fully democratizing the process and preventing any single entity from dominating. Unlike the current Proposer-Builder Separation (PBS) method, which relies on a centralized builder pool, the new system uses BFT to mitigate mempool variance and ensure robustness. MEV, which involves profit-driven manipulation strategies like arbitrage and front-running, has been a concern for ordinary network users, as it undermines Ethereum's decentralized ethos.

Under Malik672's proposal, all Ethereum clients would construct blocks using a cryptographically random algorithm, preventing the manipulation tactics that currently impact users. This solution aligns with Ethereum's trustless roots and remains compatible with Danksharding's blob requirements, a solution aimed at scaling Ethereum layer 2s. Additionally, the proposal could potentially reduce slot times from 12 seconds to approximately 6 to 8 seconds.

This development comes as Ethereum developers and researchers have agreed to deploy upgrades at a faster pace, as announced on February 13, to accelerate Ethereum's technical roadmap. The ongoing efforts to enhance Ethereum's infrastructure reflect the community's commitment to maintaining its decentralized nature while improving efficiency and scalability.

data.title: CryptoPunks Market Faces Flash Loan Exploitation
data.description: According to Foresight News, Garga.eth, co-founder of Yuga Labs, has reported that individuals are exploiting flash loans to manipulate bids in the CryptoPunks market. Recently, several CryptoPunk NFTs have received substantial bids, with CryptoPunk 5822 attracting an offer of 8,888 ETH, approximately valued at $19,587,819.67.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Updates Twitter Profile With World Of Warcraft Image
data.description: According to Foresight News, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has updated his Twitter profile picture to feature a character from the popular game World of Warcraft. His Twitter bio now reads, "I choose balance. First-level balance." Previously, Buterin's profile picture was from the Milady NFT series.

data.title: US Crypto Market Fear & Greed Index Down, Impact on Bitcoin
data.description:
Investors in the crypto market trading in the US financial landscape are showing signs of panic, as seen in the mainstream Fear & Greed Index. This index fell as low as 18 points, its lowest since August 2024. Such a state is usually connected with substantial sell-offs and declining investor confidence in the crypto and […]
